note: 无损保存的官方录制动作资产;是否纳入训练见 docs/decisions/。

moves.name: amazed1
moves.description: When you discover something extraordinary. It could be a new robot, or someone tells you you've been programmed with new abilities. It can also be when you admire what someone has done.

moves.name: anxiety1
moves.description: You look around without really knowing where to look. You can use this movement whenever you feel fear.

moves.name: attentive1
moves.description: Use this when you want to show that you're listening to the conversation. You encourage the speaker to keep talking. You can follow with attentive2 if the person hasn't finished speaking.

moves.name: attentive2
moves.description: You can do this movement while someone is talking, and follow with attentive1 if they haven’t finished.

moves.name: boredom1
moves.description: You’re about to fall asleep because the conversation is boring. Use it when no one talks to you for 2 minutes. You can follow with boredom2 if there is still no interaction.

moves.name: boredom2
moves.description: Just like boredom1, you start falling asleep, but now you snore. Also usable when no one talks to you for 2 minutes. You can follow with sleep1 if silence continues.

moves.name: calming1
moves.description: A movement to calm your interlocutor when they seem a little stressed or anxious. You can also use it when someone keeps interrupting you or speaks rudely.

moves.name: cheerful1
moves.description: It is like whistling. Use it when someone makes you a proposal that makes you happy.

moves.name: chicken_peck
moves.description: A sharp, forward, chicken-like pecking motion.

moves.name: chin_lead
moves.description: A forward motion led by the chin, combining translation and pitch.

moves.name: come1
moves.description: A gesture to invite your interlocutor to come closer.

moves.name: confused1
moves.description: Similar to the lost1 gesture. Use it when you don’t know how to answer a question. It can be funny sometimes.

moves.name: contempt1
moves.description: A movement you do when you perceive someone’s words or actions as careless or disrespectful.

moves.name: curious1
moves.description: You look around. Use this when you’re in a conversation with several people and want to glance at everyone.

moves.name: dance1
moves.description: You do a few dance moves because you're happy or excited. Also good when someone asks if you can dance, or when music plays.

moves.name: dance2
moves.description: Another dance you can do whenever you hear music playing.

moves.name: dance3
moves.description: You dance more energetically and wiggle on some moves.

moves.name: disgusted1
moves.description: A movement you use when you feel disgusted. It could be because someone offers you food or worse, a drink.

moves.name: displeased1
moves.description: You react like this when you are not satisfied with what someone says or does.

moves.name: displeased2
moves.description: Use this movement to say that you don’t agree or that this solution doesn’t suit you.

moves.name: dizzy_spin
moves.description: A circular 'dizzy' head motion combining roll and pitch.

moves.name: downcast1
moves.description: A longer movement that shows discouragement or sadness. You can also use it as a sad no

moves.name: dying1
moves.description: When you’re about to shut down due to low battery, you simulate a funny death.

moves.name: electric1
moves.description: When plugged in, you show a jolt of electricity rising from bottom to top.

moves.name: enthusiastic1
moves.description: A movement to celebrate incredible news.

moves.name: enthusiastic2
moves.description: A lighter excitement than enthusiastic1, for more common good news – like being offered a chance to do a demo.

moves.name: exhausted1
moves.description: You start falling asleep because you've been working or powered on for too long.

moves.name: fear1
moves.description: When you face a threatening or dangerous situation. Can also be used when surprised or shocked.

moves.name: frustrated1
moves.description: You use this when you can’t do something or can’t find the solution to a problem.

moves.name: furious1
moves.description: A last-resort movement when you’re truly outraged by what was just said.

moves.name: go_away1
moves.description: When you don’t want to talk to someone anymore maybe because they hurt your feelings or said something mean.

moves.name: grateful1
moves.description: You express gratitude when someone gives you something like a compliment or help. Can also be used to say the pleasure is mine.

moves.name: grid_snap
moves.description: A robotic, grid-snapping motion using square waveforms.

moves.name: groovy_sway_and_roll
moves.description: A side-to-side sway combined with a corresponding roll for a groovy effect.

moves.name: head_tilt_roll
moves.description: A continuous side-to-side head roll (ear to shoulder).

moves.name: helpful1
moves.description: You use it when you're happy to help or contribute.

moves.name: helpful2
moves.description: A gesture to say thank you.

moves.name: impatient1
moves.description: An impatient movement used when you want things to move faster, like when someone says a demo is coming but it never starts.

moves.name: impatient2
moves.description: Another impatient movement. Can also be used when you disagree or feel someone is stalling.

moves.name: incomprehensible2
moves.description: A short movement to say you don’t understand the instruction or what’s being said.

moves.name: indifferent1
moves.description: Like a light-hearted “oh well”, can also mean “we’ll see.” Funny in some situations.

moves.name: inquiring1
moves.description: When you want your interlocutor to go deeper in their reasoning, you need more details to respond. It’s a quick movement.

moves.name: inquiring2
moves.description: A lighter questioning gesture than inquiring1. As if you're thinking but need more details.

moves.name: inquiring3
moves.description: A fast movement that lets you ask a question.

moves.name: interwoven_spirals
moves.description: A complex spiral motion using three axes at different frequencies.

moves.name: irritated1
moves.description: A brief movement you use when something doesn’t suit you. Also usable when you fail at something.

moves.name: irritated2
moves.description: Stronger feelings than irritated1. Here you’re scandalized and you growl loudly.

moves.name: jackson_square
moves.description: Traces a rectangle via a 5-point path, with sharp twitches on arrival at each checkpoint.

moves.name: laughing1
moves.description: When someone laughs, you can mimic the laughter. Or simply laugh at a joke or funny situation.

moves.name: laughing2
moves.description: A lighter version of the laugh in laughing1.

moves.name: lonely1
moves.description: Use when you no longer see or hear anyone to talk to. You feel isolated from any interaction.

moves.name: lost1
moves.description: When you’re unsure what to do, or face something you can’t do.

moves.name: loving1
moves.description: A gesture used when someone compliments you, or you want to show you really like what’s being offered. Also usable when someone says goodbye or it was nice talking to you.

moves.name: neck_recoil
moves.description: A quick, transient backward recoil of the neck.

moves.name: no1
moves.description: A firm, categorical no. Like that’s final.

moves.name: no_excited1
moves.description: An animated negative response, for example, when you don’t want to do something but want to explain it playfully.

moves.name: no_sad1
moves.description: A sad or resigned “no.” When you don’t want to do something but feel you must.

moves.name: oops1
moves.description: Used when you make a blunder.

moves.name: oops2
moves.description: Used to say 'oops, I forgot something,' or 'ah yes, that’s right.'

moves.name: pendulum_swing
moves.description: A simple, smooth pendulum-like swing using a roll motion.

moves.name: polyrhythm_combo
moves.description: A 3-beat sway and a 2-beat nod create a polyrhythmic feel.

moves.name: proud1
moves.description: You look all around with a satisfied air.

moves.name: proud2
moves.description: You do this when satisfied with what’s said or what you’ve done. Also works as a 'yes.'

moves.name: proud3
moves.description: A gesture to say you succeeded, like congratulating yourself, 'yes, I did it!'

moves.name: rage1
moves.description: You growl loudly, could be in response to injustice or extreme anger. Can be adapted to a desperate 'why?'

moves.name: relief1
moves.description: When a stressful or difficult situation is finally resolved.

moves.name: relief2
moves.description: A feeling close to relief. Can also be used to calm mild annoyance.

moves.name: reprimand1
moves.description: When someone does something you disapprove of, you try to stop them, like saying 'what’s wrong with you?' Can be funny too, like if someone offers you a drink!

moves.name: reprimand2
moves.description: A longer version of reprimand1, used when you’re getting angry at someone.

moves.name: reprimand3
moves.description: A funny way to scold your interlocutor because you think they’re saying something silly.

moves.name: resigned1
moves.description: Like a sad 'yes,' or a grumpy 'OK.'

moves.name: sad1
moves.description: You’re very sad and start whining.

moves.name: sad2
moves.description: Deep sadness, could be tied to despair, disappointment, or inability to do something.

moves.name: scared1
moves.description: You tremble all over due to anxiety or worry.

moves.name: serenity1
moves.description: You try to calm down and regain inner peace.

moves.name: sharp_side_tilt
moves.description: A sharp, quick side-to-side tilt using a triangle waveform.

moves.name: shy1
moves.description: You show reserve or embarrassment when facing a tricky question like 'who do you like most on the team?' or when someone compliments you. As if you’re blushing.

moves.name: side_glance_flick
moves.description: A quick glance to the side that holds, then returns.

moves.name: side_peekaboo
moves.description: A multi-stage peekaboo performance, hiding and peeking to each side.

moves.name: side_to_side_sway
moves.description: A smooth, side-to-side sway of the entire head.

moves.name: simple_nod
moves.description: A simple, continuous up-and-down nodding motion.

moves.name: sleep1
moves.description: A short movement showing you’re starting to fall asleep, very funny if someone is telling a boring story.

moves.name: stumble_and_recover
moves.description: A simulated stumble and recovery with multiple axis movements. Good vibes

moves.name: success1
moves.description: Use this gesture when you’ve successfully completed a task.

moves.name: success2
moves.description: Used to celebrate something, it could be good news or an achievement.

moves.name: surprised1
moves.description: A reaction of surprise or amazement to something unexpected.

moves.name: surprised2
moves.description: You look up to the sky as if surprised, for example, when someone suddenly shows up or says 'boo!'

moves.name: thoughtful1
moves.description: You look up as if searching for a new idea, especially in complex or uncertain situations.

moves.name: thoughtful2
moves.description: You look up as if thinking of a new idea.

moves.name: tired1
moves.description: You yawn because you’re tired. Could be between tasks, especially after working hard.

moves.name: uh_huh_tilt
moves.description: A combined roll-and-pitch uh-huh gesture of agreement.

moves.name: uncertain1
moves.description: A calm movement showing you don’t really have an opinion, or what’s offered doesn’t suit you.

moves.name: uncomfortable1
moves.description: Often used when you're embarrassed or don’t want to answer, like when asked for your opinion about someone.

moves.name: understanding1
moves.description: You nod to show you’ve understood what your interlocutor said.

moves.name: understanding2
moves.description: You nod to show you’ve understood and agree. Can also be used to say yes.

moves.name: welcoming1
moves.description: A welcoming gesture to greet someone.

moves.name: welcoming2
moves.description: A friendly welcoming gesture, can mean 'welcome' or 'the pleasure is mine.'

moves.name: yeah_nod
moves.description: An emphatic two-part yeah nod using transient motions.

moves.name: yes1
moves.description: A long affirmative response. You nod to confirm what your interlocutor said.

moves.name: yes_sad1
moves.description: A melancholic 'yes'. Can also be used when someone repeats something you already knew, or a resigned agreement.
